entries.currentBehavior: requires the Provider write contract, applies a ProviderWriteDraft through upsert_provider_draft_command, commits clone/validate/persist/swap, and returns a redacted ProviderView
entries.targetCommand: retain provider_commands::create_provider over the shared commit coordinator
entries.risk: a compatibility endpoint could bypass CredentialPatch or return the stored secret-bearing record

entries.currentBehavior: checks path/body identity, requires the Provider write contract, and uses the same ProviderWriteDraft command and redacted response as create
entries.targetCommand: retain provider_commands::update_provider over the shared commit coordinator
entries.risk: create and update semantics could drift or allow a resource id substitution

entries.currentBehavior: requires explicit preview/apply mode; preview validates without writing, while apply binds the same typed batch and current snapshot to a token checked inside the commit coordinator
entries.risk: a compatibility import path could bypass preview binding, batch validation, or CredentialPatch handling

entries.currentBehavior: uses profileId as the authoritative identity and writes a typed resource through the shared command; mutable preset name remains a read-only request fallback during the compatibility window
entries.targetCommand: retain provider_commands::create_from_profile_id and remove only the name fallback after the compatibility gate
entries.risk: removing the deprecated name fallback before two stable releases and 14 days would break an older Web bundle

entries.currentBehavior: requires the same write contract and delegates both compatibility commands to upsert_provider_draft_command with CredentialPatch
entries.risk: future invoke compatibility changes could diverge from REST validation or credential semantics

entries.currentBehavior: uses the commit coordinator and stores per-app presentation order in the ProviderStore top-level order map without changing Provider revision
entries.risk: a future compatibility change could reintroduce sortIndex mutation into runtime-bearing Provider records

entries.currentBehavior: delegates to delete_provider_command, which holds the reference mutation gate and rejects Share/current-provider references without mutating them
entries.targetCommand: retain provider_commands::delete_provider with reference preview and conflict response
entries.risk: a future force-delete path could bypass the reference gate or revive cross-store cascade side effects

entries.currentBehavior: decodes S1 without writing; legacy classification and model normalization are prepared only in the in-memory runtime view
entries.targetCommand: retain pure S1 decoder plus LegacyRuntimeView
entries.risk: a future loader normalization could silently mutate persisted Provider behavior during startup

entries.currentBehavior: startup performs read-only inspection; mutation is available only through the explicit admin preview/apply command
entries.risk: calling the apply function from ordinary startup would restore an implicit save-on-load migration

entries.currentBehavior: returns discovery observations without writing; merge=true is rejected and users must explicitly save a selected model through the typed Provider command
entries.risk: a future convenience merge could couple untrusted network observations to Provider configuration and revision
evidence: src/api/providers.rs::automatic model discovery merge is retired

entries.currentBehavior: a detached commit coordinator materializes a candidate, validates and compiles RuntimePlan, seals credentials, atomically persists in the store's current S1/S2 format, reconciles post-rename errors, then swaps live state; fresh stores start as S2
entries.risk: new Provider writers could bypass S2 sealing or weaken the disk/live commit-point reconciliation

entries.currentBehavior: preserves S1 only for an existing unmigrated installation, writes encrypted guarded S2 for fresh or explicitly migrated stores, and never silently downgrades S2
entries.targetCommand: retain format-aware ProviderStore codec until the compatibility removal gate
entries.risk: a raw serializer or format reset could disclose plaintext credentials or make an S2 file readable as S1

entries.currentBehavior: runs only as an explicit offline command under the data-directory process lock, performs read-only preflight and RuntimePlan parity, snapshots S1, atomically cuts over to S2, and permits audited snapshot rollback or cleanup
entries.targetCommand: retain explicit provider storage migration commands
entries.risk: running migration beside a live Server or deleting the rollback snapshot implicitly could corrupt the store or remove the downgrade path

entries.currentBehavior: stages all files and validates typed stores, matching Account/Provider keys, authenticated S2 credential decryption, reserved sentinels, and Provider RuntimePlan compilation before live replacement; files remain sequentially replaced by the accepted same-installation restore boundary
entries.risk: an apply-time filesystem failure can require the generated pre-restore snapshot for rollback

entries.currentBehavior: preloads every typed store before ordered in-memory swaps under the Provider commit lock; cross-store online transactional visibility is explicitly outside the product boundary
entries.risk: concurrent readers may observe an ordered reload transition, so restore remains an explicit administrative operation

entries.currentBehavior: returns redacted ProviderView records without reusable credentials or ciphertext
entries.risk: a DTO regression could disclose credentials or make an ordinary export reusable as a secret bundle
